anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Atman and anatta share a de-identification role, but the shared role may be too thin to matter and too historically entangled to count as independent convergence.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence in the upgrade bar until the method separates thin functional similarity, doctrinal sameness, and provenance. It supports a three-verdict protocol but blocks any claim of strong convergence.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: If the positive verdict confirms only a generic role such as destabilizing ordinary identity, it may inflate trivial similarities. If a stronger role is required, metaphysical incompatibility and historical contact may keep the verdict undecidable. The same issue appears in Gaudapada and Nagarjuna: low strain may reflect borrowing or polemic, not independent support.

claim_units: A comparison method must allow more than one verdict.
The current translation-strain frontier is biased toward downgrade-only gates.
Before decomposition, analysts should state what a surviving shared role would look like and what would confirm it.
After decomposition, the method should allow confirmed, false, or undecidable verdicts.
A method that never upgrades confidence should be renamed as a skepticism protocol, not a convergence test.
The human-condition target is reflective seekers whose comparison habit produces cynicism, meaning loss, digital comparison, and isolation.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: A confirm, expose, undecidable protocol will predict both successful and failed transfers better than critique-only culturalist analysis or success-assuming borrowing analysis. It should identify partial transplants that work because role, authority, and local institutions survive adaptation.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Before analyzing a proposed legal transplant or policy transfer, analysts pre-register what successful transfer would look like, then assign confirmed fit, false fit, or undecidable, while coding provenance and local adaptation separately.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If the protocol only predicts failure, cannot distinguish successful adaptation from post-hoc rationalization, or adds no value beyond existing Dolowitz-Marsh, Watson, Legrand, and policy-transfer frameworks.

exact_claim: A convergence test is not a real test if it can only downgrade apparent likeness. Translation-strain analysis should pre-register what would count as a surviving shared role, then permit three verdicts: confirmed partial convergence, false match, or undecidable. A procedure that never raises confidence is measuring its own skepticism rather than convergence.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Across cases such as Atman and anatta, apophatic theology and Buddhist emptiness, compassion practices, ritual confession, and ethical restraint, coders produce a non-zero rate of confirmed partial shared-role verdicts with acceptable agreement.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: A pre-registered upgrade condition should produce some confirmed partial convergences in a diverse blind sample, not only downgrades.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Blind inter-rater trial comparing downgrade-scale strain analysis with confirm, expose, undecidable scoring.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If trained coders still produce almost no confirmations, or confirmations are too thin to predict anything, the method remains a skepticism engine or the upgrade bar is cosmetic.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Coders using confirmed, false, and undecidable reach higher agreement and make better held-out predictions about translation strain, warning profiles, practitioner transfer limits, and likely misuse.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The three-verdict scheme should improve decision quality over a downgrade-magnitude scale.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source comparison task with held-out practitioner and manual evidence.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: No better agreement or predictive power than ordinary strain scores, or worse false-positive convergence.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Practitioners trained in one tradition can recognize a bounded role in another tradition, such as de-identification or repentance, while still naming non-transferable metaphysical and authority differences.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Confirmed partial convergence should predict limited practice intelligibility without implying doctrinal sameness.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Practitioner interviews and blinded scenario classification with Buddhist, Advaita, Christian, Sufi, and comparative theology respondents.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If practitioners either reject all shared-role claims as misleading or turn them into broad sameness, the protocol cannot hold its middle verdict.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Some confirmed shared roles will be historically dependent, some independent, and some uncertain. Coders can distinguish role survival from borrowing status.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Provenance coding should be orthogonal to positive shared-role scoring.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Gaudapada and Nagarjuna, Christian apophasis and Buddhist emptiness, Pure Land and grace, plus no-contact control cases.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: If coders cannot separate role similarity from historical influence, every low-strain case becomes ambiguous and the upgrade verdict loses meaning.

literature_search_queries: Popper falsifiability corroboration test basic statements confirm refute comparison method
Bayesian confirmation theory evidence raises probability hypothesis test can confirm and disconfirm
Deborah Mayo severe testing non severe test always passes bad evidence no test
comparative method religion Smith Freiberger redescription rectification theory formation positive findings
comparative theology Clooney truth seeking learning from other traditions transformation
Gaudapada Nagarjuna non origination Buddhist influence provenance comparison

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate applies the test-risk structure to comparative-religion scoring, and specifically to an internal downgrade-only translation-strain method.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major downgrade for philosophical novelty; leaves a useful domain-specific protocol.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close on a real test having possible outcomes that would count against or bear out a theory. Popper's demarcation frame already requires a claim to risk possible falsifiers and permits corroborating observations.
near_neighbors.source: Karl Popper, Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/spr2013/entries/popper/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate translates probability-raising into a pre-registered shared-role verdict for religious comparison.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the core logic as known confirmation theory.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on evidence confirming a hypothesis when it raises the probability of that hypothesis. The candidate's demand that decomposition sometimes raise confidence is a qualitative version of this.
near_neighbors.source: Bayesian confirmation theory, Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://plato.stanford.edu/archives/spr2012/entries/epistemology-bayesian/

near_neighbors.difference: Mayo usually targets tests that always pass. The candidate targets a comparison device that always fails likeness.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong prior art for the instrument-critique, but the inversion is a useful adaptation.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close structural near-neighbor: evidence counts only when it comes from a real severe test, and a procedure designed to get the same result regardless is bad evidence or no test.
near_neighbors.source: Deborah Mayo severe testing discussion, example https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10104935/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate applies this to whether a comparative method can ever generate confirmed convergence.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Lowers novelty of the method-health criterion.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on methods and research programmes needing progressive content, novel predictions, and confirmed successes rather than only protective adjustments.
near_neighbors.source: Imre Lakatos, Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy, https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/lakatos/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds a pre-registered positive-verdict bar and asks whether a specific strain sheet is downgrade-only.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Makes the candidate an extension of comparative-method discipline, not a discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on comparison as a configured method involving selection, description, juxtaposition, redescription, rectification, and theory formation. Freiberger already treats comparison as more than debunking likeness.
near_neighbors.source: Oliver Freiberger, Elements of a Comparative Methodology, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/9/2/38

near_neighbors.difference: Smith prizes the gap and redescription but does not provide a confirm, expose, undecidable scoring protocol with a pre-registered upgrade bar.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: The candidate has some procedural specificity but not high theoretical novelty.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on manipulating similarity and difference for a stated cognitive end, avoiding superficial sameness, and using comparison creatively.
near_neighbors.source: Jonathan Z. Smith, In Comparison a Magic Dwells, https://classics.osu.edu/sites/classics.osu.edu/files/Magic_Dwells.pdf, and Patton/Ray new-comparativism discussion summarized at https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/9/2/39

near_neighbors.difference: Comparative theology is tradition-grounded and constructive, while this candidate is a neutral scoring-control rule.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades originality of the positive-verdict demand.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on comparison as genuine learning that can transform one's prior position, not merely expose false similarity.
near_neighbors.source: Comparative theology, example Rose Drew, Challenging Truths, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/3/4/1041, and overview of Clooney's comparative theology at https://www.bc.edu/content/bc-web/schools/morrissey/departments/theology/areas-of-study/comparative-theology/comparative-theology-a-bibliographic-review.html

near_neighbors.difference: Mill's methods are causal-inference tools, not translation-strain verdict rules for religious convergence.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Shows the symmetry principle is old in comparative reasoning.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close on comparison requiring both agreement and difference structures rather than one-sided negative sorting.
near_neighbors.source: John Stuart Mill's methods of agreement and difference, overview in qualitative comparison at https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6655636/

near_neighbors.difference: It adds an internal instrument-quality test: could this procedure ever raise confidence?
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Meaningful local extension, but it is dependent on the prior Lumenary cluster.
near_neighbors.overlap: The candidate directly critiques these records' subtractive accumulation of gates.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary records: Convergence as Translation Strain, Translation Strain as a Load Test for Convergence, First Ask If a Path Agrees With Itself, and provenance-gate records, supplied project memory

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as doctrine. Treat as a method-control protocol for translation-strain scoring.

Build the smallest codebook: claim unit, proposed shared role, positive upgrade bar, decomposition result, verdict confirmed or false or undecidable, provenance status, metaphysical remainder, practice-transfer limit, warning, and confidence.
Run an inter-rater test against the current downgrade-scale method. Require coders to write the upgrade bar before seeing detailed decompositions.

Use source-stratified cases: Chandogya and SN 22.59, Brihadaranyaka and SN 22.59, Gaudapada and Nagarjuna, apophatic theology and Buddhist emptiness, Pure Land and grace, compassion practices, and a low-variance ritual instruction.
Add a provenance gate so confirmed shared role, direct borrowing, shared milieu, common translator, polemic, and independent recurrence are coded separately.
Add a thinness control. A shared role must predict some practitioner-relevant understanding, warning, or transfer limit, otherwise it is too generic to count as confirmation.
Run practitioner interviews for cases where decomposition raised confidence, not only cases where it broke false similarity.

For human-condition use, keep the practice narrow: stable over-skeptical readers only. Exclude OCD, scrupulosity, acute crisis, under-critical perennialism, and people using comparison to avoid belonging or commitment.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.60 to 0.50. The core logic is strongly anticipated by falsifiability, confirmation theory, severe testing, progressive research-programme criteria, and comparative method. The valuable contribution is a local repair to Lumenary's translation-strain instrument: pre-register the positive shared-role bar and require a non-downgrade verdict to be possible. Do not promote above extended until inter-rater and held-out prediction tests show gain over existing comparison methods.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Originality falls if scholars identify it as ordinary falsifiability, Bayesian confirmation, severe testing, Millian agreement and difference, Freiberger-style comparison, Smithian redescription, or comparative theology with different labels. It also falls if practitioners say the positive verdict is either trivial or dangerous.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Support rises if experts agree that current strain scoring is one-sided, can name cases where decomposition should raise confidence in a bounded role, and find that the pre-registered upgrade bar prevents both cynical debunking and false unity.
questions: When you compare two traditions, do you state in advance what would count as a confirmed partial likeness, or do you mainly look for what breaks the match?
Does the confirm, expose, undecidable verdict change your comparison practice, or is it standard comparative method, Bayesian confirmation, severe testing, or comparative theology in new language?
Can you name a real case where decomposition raised your confidence in a bounded shared role without collapsing doctrinal difference?
Can you name a case where forcing a positive verdict would create perennialist false unity or appropriation?
Would this protocol help over-skeptical seekers become more connected and committed, or would it feed more analysis and delay practice?
relevant_practitioners: clinicians familiar with rumination, OCD, scrupulosity, cynicism, and meaning loss

primary_pair: Chandogya Upanishad 6.8.7, https://shlokam.org/texts/Chandogya-6-8-7, and Brihadaranyaka 3.7.23, read against SN 22.59, https://suttacentral.net/sn22.59/en/sujato
Gaudapada's Mandukya Karika non-origination claims, read against Nagarjuna's MMK non-arising structure as a provenance-pressure case
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Moderate to high. The idea may rightly expose a one-sided internal method, but it can overcorrect into false positive perennialism. A shared role can become so thin that almost any two practices confirm. Human-condition fit is real but narrow: it may help over-skeptical, stable, reflective seekers who debunk every likeness and become isolated. It is not for OCD or scrupulosity doubt loops, under-critical perennialists, people using comparison to avoid commitment, or public teachers making authority claims.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Read Popper, Bayesian confirmation theory, Deborah Mayo on severe testing, Lakatos on progressive and degenerating programmes, Freiberger and Smith on comparison, Paden and Patton on new comparativism, Clooney and Rose Drew on comparative theology, Davidson-style interpretationist comparison, Sharma on Atman and anatta, and specialist work on Gaudapada and Madhyamaka before raising novelty. The candidate also needs inter-rater testing, not only philosophical analogy.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Chandogya's repeated instruction authorizes identity with subtle essence and Self; Brihadaranyaka's unseen knower language supports a non-objectifiable witness reading; SN 22.59 applies not-mine, not-I, not-self to the five aggregates, including consciousness. A thin shared role survives: ordinary identity is destabilized. But the authorized remainder differs sharply: Upanishadic recognition and Buddhist non-ownership are not the same doctrine. The Gaudapada and Nagarjuna case adds a second pressure: low logical strain can reflect historical contact or polemical borrowing rather than independent recurrence.

title: A Test Must Be Able to Say Yes
unlike_statement: Unlike Popper, Bayesian confirmation theory, Mayo, Lakatos, Freiberger, Smith, comparative theology, Millian comparison, and prior Lumenary translation-strain gates, this candidate's specific contribution is a local scoring repair: before decomposing a religious likeness, write the positive shared-role condition and upgrade bar, then require the method to permit confirmed, false, and undecidable verdicts, with provenance coded separately.
